layman_headline: Market fear is extreme but no clean entry presents itself — patience is the play.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin is bouncing back toward $66,000 with ETF inflows picking up, and the broader market is showing green. However, the market mood is still 'Extreme Fear' at 25/100 and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.7%, meaning altcoins are still getting crushed relative to Bitcoin. Every swing candidate on the watchlist has significant flaws — either already ran up too much, has terrible chart structure, or conflicting signals. Rushing in now would mean chasing rallies or catching falling knives.
layman_action: Wait for a cleaner setup. If Ethereum pulls back toward $1,850 with RSI dipping below 35, that would be a much better entry point. Keep cash ready — extreme fear environments eventually produce great opportunities, but timing matters.
expert_reasoning: No candidate offers a high-conviction swing entry right now. ETH is the strongest fundamental pick with RSI at 39.4 approaching oversold territory, but chart bias is -0.70 (bearish structure, below all MAs) and it's already bounced 8.2% in 7 days — entering now means poor risk/reward at 1.41x. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers pattern, but the R/R is only 0.38x with resistance at $0.40 just 7.4% away after a 23% weekly run and 51.7% 5-day momentum — classic chase scenario. DEXE data is internally contradictory (price shows -40% 24h but RSI is 71 overbought, chart says $36.27 but price field says $20.42) making it untradeable. BONK at -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows is a falling knife. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to retest $1,850 support or for a proper MACD bullish cross confirmation.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.7% means alts continue to underperform even if BTC rallies — capital rotation to alts may not happen
Extreme Fear at 25 can persist or worsen — this is not a reliable bottom signal by itself, just context
Oil near $90 creates macro headwinds that could pressure risk assets including crypto if equity markets sell off
watch_list: BTC breaking and holding above $67,000 — would confirm range breakout and likely pull alts higher
ETH RSI dropping below 35 on a pullback to $1,800-$1,850 support — would be ideal swing entry zone
Fear & Greed index — any move below 20 or sharp reversal above 30 signals inflection point
market_context: Bitcoin is testing $66K with 5 straight days of ETF inflows, but the broader market remains in Extreme Fear at 25. BTC dominance at 56.7% signals capital is hiding in Bitcoin rather than flowing into alts, making altcoin swing trades riskier than usual.
context_banner: Bitcoin ETF inflows build momentum toward $66K but Extreme Fear and high BTC dominance make altcoin entries premature — wait for confirmation.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25/100 with flat trend — historically precedes opportunities but does not confirm a bottom yet.
ETH RSI at 39.4 is approaching oversold but hasn't triggered a clear reversal signal — needs to dip below 35 or show MACD bullish cross for confirmation.
Bitcoin ETFs posting 5-day inflow streak (longest since May) and BTC hitting one-month high at $66.3K — institutional demand is returning but hasn't spilled into alts yet.
Best candidate LDO shows Three White Soldiers but R/R is only 0.38x with resistance at $0.40 — classic late entry trap after 51.7% 5-day run.
Oil near $90 and US debt at $39.5 trillion create a macro liquidity tension — BTC holding $66K despite this is notable strength but adds uncertainty for risk assets.
